title: hackelia deflexa
intro: Hackelia deflexa, also known as Western Stickseed, is a perennial plant native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Hackelia deflexa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
